metadata.name: Hexaëder #125
metadata.description:
Hexaëder (cube) is a research into abstraction and a search for a balance between simplification and visual complexity.

No matter how simple and unambiguous things appear to be, in reality they are often more complex. Enjoy the simple, explore the complex.

Hexaeder has a wide variety of possible outcomes. I have put together 10 different color palettes; the number of cubes ranges from 10 to 401 (18 different grids); each cell can have 4 different background patterns; each cube can be filled in 4 distinct ways; there are 5 line colors and 16 different "cube figures"; the vignette can be black or be the color of the palette, and there are several "texture settings". This results in over 1.8 million possibilities.

For more information see: https://www.behance.net/gallery/131114761/Hexaeder-Flattened-cubes

metadata.name: PixlFlippr #483
metadata.description:
PixlFlippr is an offshoot of my "Simple Harmonic Motion" series, inspired by the complexity of life emerging through the interaction of simple 'dumb' behaviors, with intricate details emerging on multiple scales. PixlFlippr operates on a pixel level (in the form of a GLSL frag shader).

Every pixel oscillates at a single fixed frequency, slightly out-of-sync with its neighbors. There is no explicit pixel-neighborhood communication (as there would be in eg Cellular Automata), and every pixel acts autonomously, oblivious to its surroundings. Yet collectively, the entire system exhibits constantly evolving complex high level behavior on many scales, ranging from patterns & behaviors spanning a few pixels, all the way up to the level of the entire image. These are further amplified through the emergent pixel aliasing artefacts & moiré patterns. 

Also, it's trippy af. 

PS All generative mints are a seamless loop with a loop duration ranging from a few minutes to over an hour. So stick around & watch it mutate!

metadata.name: Caught in a Void #108
metadata.description:
A reflection on the 571 days between the start of the COVID lockdown and quitting my job to be a generative artist.

Features include silhouette type, color palette, and fill effect.

p5.js

metadata.name: OpenBLOCK #167
metadata.description:
OpenBLOCK is a collection of open architectures. 
An architecture machine, creating endless open configurations. 
Each minted OpenBLOCK is a part of the whole, an open form, constructed using a kit of parts in various deterministic tectonic configurations, lights, and moods.

OpenBLOCK Generative Token by @grasser_alex 2021

metadata.name: Garden, Monoliths #137
metadata.description:
"Garden, Monoliths" is the result of an algorithm that started in April 2021. It was called "Grass.js".
Spring was blooming all around the house, taking over the stones and remnants of ancient battles that stood here. Inspiration was everywhere.
The initial goal was to use mathematical formulas for creating patterns inspired by the density of nature, that could be used by a pen plotter, so it kept focusing on outputting vector lines. This history and practice produced a graphical style based on line strokes, plus occasionally some transparent, filled shapes.
Each artwork will be either "Garden" or "Monolith", for they are variations of two existing designs from the workshop.

metadata.name: Dragons #292

metadata.name: {Lumina} #1642
metadata.description:
The restitution of a breath
Your breath is your artwork

{Lumina} is part of the installation Σ Lumina taking place at Musée d’Orsay. A free live minting is scheduled from February 13th to March 10th, 2024. 

Breath, the vital essence, embodies our interconnection. It carries our shared heritage. A medium that helps Σ Lumina to unite the physical and the digital, the living with the coded. The voices of Orsay resonate within us, their breath becoming ours, landscaping our common creation.


Art curators of {Le Code d’Orsay}:
Christophe Leribault,
Président de l’Établissement public du musée d’Orsay et du musée de l’Orangerie
Virginie Donzeaud, Administratrice générale adjointe

16x16
HTML, js, Webgl
Location: Musée d’Orsay

metadata.name: Splash Funk #3
metadata.description:
The Splash Funk collection is based on moving particles in a 3 dimensional space that have as their starting position a point on either a sphere, a cube or a pyramid. The particles will then follow an algorithmically defined movement which is captured after a certain time period. 

Generative Art by pifragile.
pifragile.com
@pifragile
